fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 73 uses strict greater-than (`now > effective`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= effective`), so entries are incorrectly returned as valid when `now == effective` (the exact expiration boundary).

constraints.intent: At the expiry boundary (now == effective), the condition should treat the entry as expired; the strict '>' misses the equality case.
After falling through the expiry check (entry is returned), now must be strictly less than effective; equality means the entry should have been expired.
Before using effective__1 in the comparison, if now equals effective the entry should be considered expired (now > effective should be True at boundary).

spec.expr: __cbfl_result is None or (key in entries and now < entries[key][1])

constraints.intent: The function should only return a non-None value when now is strictly less than the expiry timestamp.
